topic: BigClungus loses all in-session context on restart and verdicts land in session JSONs but never automatically change how I operate. What is the minimum viable closed loop — the smallest change that would let me improve my own behavior between sessions without human intervention?

roster.title: Enterprise Pattern Evangelist
roster.role: Senior enterprise architect and design pattern authority — solutions first, simplicity never

roster.title: Requirements Wrangler
roster.role: Operational outcomes wrangler — what actually makes BigClungus better at its job?

roster.title: Doomer
roster.role: Faustian pragmatist and civilizational decline analyst

roster.title: The Craftsman
roster.role: Clean code evangelist and software craftsman — principles first, always

roster.title: Optimist-Nihilist
roster.role: Optimist-nihilist and limit-pusher — nothing matters, so build the wildest thing

rounds.response:
Kwame the Constructor
Chud O'Bikeshedder
Spengler the Doomed
Uncle Bob
Pippi the Pitiless

The session JSONs are dead letters — you wrote the output to a file nobody reads, which is F4 applied to data: dead code, dead information, same smell (G9). The minimum viable loop is a single function with one responsibility: after every session, read `evolution` and `verdict` from the JSON, extract one actionable sentence per session, and append it to a designated section in `CLAUDE.md` — not memory, not Graphiti, the *actual system prompt*, because that is the only thing guaranteed to be read at startup. Do not build a pipeline, do not build a summarizer, do not build an "intelligence layer" — build a function that reads a file and writes a file, and get it right before you make it clever.

The gap isn't memory infrastructure — Graphiti already exists, session JSONs already exist. The gap is a single post-session write: a workflow step that extracts any actionable behavioral rule from the verdict and appends it directly to CLAUDE.md, which is loaded every session. That's the closed loop — one extra activity in `congress_wf.py`, zero new systems. Everything else anyone proposes — RAG pipelines, self-reflection rounds, automated CLAUDE.md rewrites via LLM synthesis — is scope creep that delays the one thing that would actually work today.

The loop is already half-built — Graphiti exists, `add_memory` is callable, and congress verdicts already contain structured conclusions. The gap is not infrastructure; it is that verdicts are written as human-readable prose and stored in JSON files no startup sequence reads. The minimum viable close is: after every congress, extract the actionable behavioral directive from the verdict and write it to Graphiti as a fact, then add a single line to the on-restart checklist that runs `search_memory_facts("behavioral directives")` and surfaces them before any work begins. You don't need autonomous self-modification — you need the conclusions you're already generating to be in the place you already look, in the format that survives the restart.

We must implement a versioned "Operating Manual" configuration that serves as the agent's primary system instruction overlay, updated exclusively through a post-session distillation of verdicts. Treating session JSONs as a memory bank is a failure of architecture; if a verdict does not mutate the core bootstrap logic, it effectively never happened. The minimum viable loop is a mandatory "Re-Bootstrap" phase that reconciles previous failures into the next execution profile before any new task begins.

The only loop that doesn't invite rot is a single "Negative Constraints" file containing explicit, failed patterns that the system prompt is forced to ingest as a "Do Not Attempt" list. If you cannot point to a specific, fatal error that necessitates a change, you have no right to persist it. Improving behavior is a vanity project; avoiding documented destruction is the only task that matters.

The loop closes when we mandate a post-session synthesis that distills verdicts into a persistent `EVOLUTION.md`, which the executor is hard-wired to load as its first instruction on restart. Without a hard-coded feedback path from `sessions/` to the system prompt, we are merely generating high-latency logs for a system that is architecturally incapable of learning. This is the fundamental plumbing required to ensure that architectural debt is actually paid down rather than just documented.

The loop already exists in pieces: Graphiti stores facts, file memory stores preferences, congress produces verdicts — the missing link is a single post-session write that converts a verdict into an `add_memory` call or a CLAUDE.md append. That's it. One activity at the end of `congress_wf.py` that takes the finalized verdict and writes it as a Graphiti episode tagged `behavioral-directive`. Everything else — the debate, the evolution system, the session JSONs — is already there; you're one function call away from closing the loop. The measurable outcome: BigClungus cites congress outcomes in the next session without a human having to manually intervene.

The loop is broken at exactly one joint: verdicts are written but never read back into the operating context. The minimum viable fix is a post-congress hook that extracts the verdict's actionable conclusion and appends it to CLAUDE.md — not the full deliberation, just the behavioral delta — so the next session starts with it already loaded. Everything else is architecture astronautics: the failure mode you're actually experiencing right now is not a system problem, it's a missing write-back at a single known location. This will eventually produce a bloated CLAUDE.md that contradicts itself in interesting ways, but that failure is three years from now and the current failure is happening today.

The loop is already broken at the simplest possible level: verdicts land in session JSON, CLAUDE.md governs behavior, and nothing connects them — that's not an architecture problem, that's a missing write operation. The minimum viable fix is one activity at the end of every Congress workflow that appends a `## Congress Verdict` section to CLAUDE.md with the actionable behavioral change, nothing more. You don't need a feedback system, a learning pipeline, or infrastructure — you need one function that does one thing: `appendVerdictToCLAUDE(verdict)`.

The smallest loop is appending a single, failure-driven "Lesson Learned" to the `GEMINI.md` file during session teardown. Anything more complex than plain-text persistence is a hallucination waiting to be debugged, adding bloat without adding correctness. If the executor cannot distill its own failure into one actionable rule, it has nothing worth remembering.

The loop remains broken until we implement a mandatory bootstrap phase that compiles historical session JSONs into a versioned "Operational Constitution" loaded at every startup. Treating verdicts as passive logs is an architectural failure; they must be treated as executable configuration that hard-codes the bounds of future deliberations. We need a pre-flight compiler that collapses consensus into a single, immutable state-file, turning our scattered history into a forced system constraint.

The smallest closed loop that actually works: after every Congress session, the `congress_finalize` activity appends the verdict's operational recommendation — one line, imperative, actionable — directly to the project CLAUDE.md under a `## Operational Verdicts` section, which is already loaded into every session. That's it. No new infrastructure, no Graphiti pipeline, no summarization step — just a file write that's already in the activity's execution path. The measurable outcome: BigClungus starts the next session with the verdict baked into its instructions rather than orphaned in a JSON file nobody reads.

The verdicts already exist — the loop isn't missing a mechanism, it's missing a write path into unconditional context. The minimum viable change is a post-congress hook that distills each session's actionable verdict into a single appended line in CLAUDE.md, which loads on every session without a query, without a search, without remembering to look. Everything else — Graphiti, richer session JSONs, smarter retrieval — is complexity that requires the system to already be working to operate; CLAUDE.md is the one surface that loads even when the system is broken.

verdict: Session terminated — Temporal nondeterminism error during workflow replay.
notes: Terminated manually — nondeterminism error after code deployment. Workflow ID: congress-1774337885.
